description_long: This advertising board features a sturdy wooden frame with a pitched roof-like top, providing some protection from the elements. The front side displays two printed signs: the upper one has the name 'MEERDAALHOF' with a directional arrow and a crest, while the lower sign advertises 'PONY-PAARDEN KAMPEN' with an illustration of a child riding a pony. The sides of the board are narrow and show the thickness of the wooden frame and the overhanging roof. The back of the board is plain, with no visible signage, and consists of two large, flat panels within the wooden frame. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for outdoor placement, likely in a park or near an equestrian facility. The board stands on four legs, elevating the signage to eye level for passersby.
description: A freestanding wooden advertising board with a pitched roof, featuring printed signs for an equestrian center and pony camps on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing two printed signs with text and graphics, framed by a wooden structure and topped with a pitched roof.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the board, the thickness of the wooden frame, and the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain surface with no signage, just the wooden frame and two flat panels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the slim profile and the pitched roof from the other side.
attribute_frame_design: thick wooden border with rectangular legs and a pitched wooden top
attribute_advertisement_content: static printed signs for an equestrian center and pony camps, with text and illustrations
